edge: The URUR dataset is introduced to facilitate the field of UHR segmentation by providing a large-scale benchmark with fine-grained annotations and high-resolution images.
The URUR dataset is further enhanced by proposing WSDNet, a more efficient framework for UHR segmentation, especially with ultra-rich context.
WSDNet is proposed as a framework for UHR segmentation, integrating multi-level Discrete Wavelet Transform and Wavelet Smooth Loss for improved performance.

These are datasets that contain more objects and instances, with objects being smaller in scale. They are related because they represent datasets used for object detection.

URUR datasets contain more objects and richer context, making the annotation process challenging due to the spatial mixture of objects.

Annotation of fine-grained UHR images is a time-consuming process due to the excessively ultra-high image resolution and chain problems caused by the ultra-rich image context among objects with drastically changing scales.
Each original UHR image with a size of 5,120×5,120 is cropped into multiple patches with a size of 1,000×1,000 for efficient and accurate annotation.
Annotators annotate the image patches separately, and the results are merged to get the final annotations relative to the original UHR images, ensuring efficiency and accuracy.
Annotators annotate image patches separately to facilitate the annotation process and improve the accuracy of the results.
Neighboring patches have a 120×1,000 pixels overlap region to guarantee consistency of annotation results and avoid boundary vanishing during cropping.
The process of merging annotation results ensures accuracy in the final annotations relative to the original UHR images.
The ISD-Net model is trained with the early manually annotated images to generate segmentation masks on the rest of the images.
Annotators adjust the masks with the help of annotation tools developed by us.

DeepGlobe and ISIC have different focuses in their datasets. DeepGlobe focuses on image annotation while ISIC focuses on lesion segmentation.
